invariantResults.evidence: Lines 49-57: The multi-paragraph note callout now has bold title '**Non-nullable AccessSettings**' on its own line (line 51) after the opening {% note %} tag (line 49), with a blank line separator. Two body paragraphs follow. This was the rejected invariant from attempt 1 and is now correctly fixed.

Three callouts used: note (line 17, important info about alternative approach), info (line 43, additional reference context), note (line 49, important behavioral constraint). Severity levels match content appropriately.
No callout contains its type name as text. Bold titles are descriptive ('Object event handlers', 'Non-nullable AccessSettings'), not 'Note' or 'Info'.

Note callout (lines 49-57) documents a non-null enforcement that throws ArgumentNullException — an important behavioral constraint warranting 'note' severity. Info callout (lines 43-47) provides supplementary reference link — appropriate for 'info'.

New content (lines 53-55): All code elements properly backticked — `AccessSettings`, `ContentAccessSettings`, `null`, `ArgumentNullException`, `ContentAccessSettings.Public()`. Pre-existing code elements also properly formatted throughout.
No code elements are pluralized directly. The phrase 'event data states' on line 53 is a natural language description, not a code element name.
No inline code spans are wrapped in bold or italics anywhere in the document.
Punctuation following formatted text is not formatted. E.g., line 53: '`ArgumentNullException`.' — period outside backticks. Lines 29-31: en dashes after bold terms are outside bold markers.
Single spaces between sentences throughout. Line 68 indentation is 4 spaces. No tabs in markdown body.
Line 53 uses U+2013 EN DASH correctly for emphasis/explanation. Lines 29-31 use the same en dash for field descriptions. No em dashes present.
Step-by-step instructions in Example section (lines 65-68) use ordered list. Unordered list for non-sequential items (lines 29-31).

'Use events to create integrations' section (lines 110-114) at end of article provides next steps with link to 'Integrate with decoupled systems'. New note adds cross-reference to Reference page (line 55).

Page is self-contained with inline links for all referenced topics: object event handlers (line 22), events reference (lines 45, 55), startup code (line 59), custom code integration (line 68), decoupled systems (line 114).
All list items begin with sentence case. Lines 29-31: bold terms start with uppercase.
Maximum list nesting is 2 levels (line 68). Does not exceed 3.
All headings use sentence case: 'Handle global events', 'Assign handlers to events', 'Example', 'Use events to create integrations'.
Verb-phrase headings use imperative mood: 'Handle', 'Assign', 'Use'.
No heading starts with 'How to'.

New content uses active voice: 'expose an AccessSettings property' (line 53), 'This property requires' (line 53), 'throws an ArgumentNullException' (line 53), 'Use ContentAccessSettings.Public()' (line 53).
Present simple tense used throughout new content: 'expose', 'requires', 'throws', 'Use', 'See'.
'please' does not appear anywhere in the document.
New note content is concise — 3 sentences covering the constraint, consequence, and fix, plus one reference sentence.
No assumption of prior reading. All referenced topics include cross-reference links.
Title 'Handle global events' does not end with -ing form.
Line 59: 'You need to register event handlers' — correctly uses 'You need to' not 'You must'.
All callouts use valid Liquid admonition tags: note, info.
Code blocks use {% code lang=csharp %} with valid lang value (lines 35, 70).
New cross-reference (line 55) uses identifier '2AOiCQ', consistent with the same identifier in the pre-existing info callout (line 45) for the same target page.
New link (line 55) uses descriptive linkText='Reference - Global system events'. No vague link text anywhere.
Line 20 uses 'Xperience API' (subsequent mention, short form). No incorrect standalone 'Kentico' for product.

additionalFindings.evidence: Headings follow consistent h2 level: '## Assign handlers to events', '## Example', '## Use events to create integrations'. No skipped levels.

New note callout (lines 49-57) follows multi-paragraph pattern with bold title matching the first note (lines 17-23). Minor whitespace difference (blank line after {% note %} in new vs. none in first) but both render correctly.
Both code blocks have language tags, consistent indentation, and complete content.
All internal links use {% page_link %} Liquid syntax. New link includes escaped hyphen in linkText per conventions.
Front matter contains required fields: title, persona, identifier, order, license, redirect_from.
All Liquid tags properly opened and closed: note/endnote, info/endinfo, code/endcode, page_link.
New content sentences are clear, concise, and scannable. Longest new sentence (~22 words on line 55) is within acceptable range.

INV-formatting-012 (should): Oxford comma missing in pre-existing text — line 20 'created, updated or deleted' and line 45 'event types and handler parameters'. Not introduced by this task; minor improvement opportunity for a future pass.

metaKnowledgeApplied.knowledgeBriefStatus: cold-start — no styleEvolutions, antiPatterns, or patterns available

summary.attemptComparison: Attempt 1 was rejected for INV-callout-002 (missing bold title in multi-paragraph note). Attempt 2 correctly adds '**Non-nullable AccessSettings**' as bold title on its own line. No new violations introduced.
